questions: Does the auto-tag workflow correctly implement all ticket requirements (trigger, version extraction, idempotency, fail-closed, PAT usage)?
Are there any correctness bugs or edge cases in the shell logic (set -euo pipefail + grep -q in if conditions, race conditions)?
Is publish.yml unchanged and are all quality gates passing?

answers.answer: Yes. The workflow triggers on push to main (no path filter), reads version via jq with explicit empty/null check and set -euo pipefail, checks tag existence via git ls-remote (remote-authoritative, works with shallow clone), creates/pushes tag only when absent (if condition on check-tag output), and uses actions/checkout@v4 with secrets.RELEASE_TOKEN PAT to enable downstream publish.yml triggering. Permissions are set to contents:read (least-privilege GITHUB_TOKEN scope). All acceptance criteria, non-negotiable invariants, and failure behaviors are correctly implemented.
evidence: Version extraction + fail-closed: lines 20-30 (jq + set -euo pipefail + empty/null check)
Tag existence check: lines 32-43 (git ls-remote + grep -q with conditional output)
Tag create/push: lines 45-52 (conditional on exists == false, set -euo pipefail)

answers.answer: No bugs found. The set -euo pipefail + grep -q pattern inside the if condition (line 37) is correct because bash set -e is explicitly exempt for commands in if test expressions. When the tag does not exist, grep -q exits 1, the if evaluates to false, and execution correctly routes to the else branch setting exists=false. Race conditions between concurrent pushes are handled safely: the second git push fails if the first already created the tag (fail-closed). A transient git ls-remote failure would cause the workflow to attempt tag creation, but git push would also fail in that scenario, maintaining fail-closed behavior.
evidence: Line 35: set -euo pipefail in check-tag step
Line 37: if git ls-remote ... | grep -q — bash set -e exempt in if conditions per POSIX spec
Lines 45-52: set -euo pipefail in create-and-push step ensures git push failure aborts the step
Line 46: if condition (exists == 'false') gates tag creation

answers.answer: Yes. publish.yml is confirmed unchanged (63 lines, same content as pre-implementation). All quality gates pass independently verified: npm run typecheck (exit 0), npm run build (exit 0), npm test (exit 0, 51/51 pass). YAML lint of auto-tag.yml also passes.
evidence: .github/workflows/publish.yml: 63 lines unchanged, triggers on push tags v*
npm test: exit code 0, 51 pass, 0 fail

statement: Code review complete. The single new file .github/workflows/auto-tag.yml (53 lines) was reviewed against all ticket requirements, acceptance criteria, non-negotiable invariants, and product spec success criteria. No issues found. The workflow correctly triggers on push to main, reads version via jq with fail-closed error handling, checks for existing tags via git ls-remote (remote-authoritative), and creates/pushes v<version> only when absent. PAT usage via actions/checkout token parameter enables downstream publish.yml triggering. Shell strict mode (set -euo pipefail) is applied in all steps. Idempotency is correctly implemented. All quality gates pass independently verified (typecheck, build, 51/51 tests). publish.yml is unchanged. No code changes made by review.
